title: The One With Two Parts (2)

jokes.text: My God, you still have your Christmas lights up?
jokes.setup: Rachel explains her friend fell taking down Christmas lights and may have broken her ankle
jokes.punchline: Hospital worker is more concerned about the late Christmas lights than the injury
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - audience expects concern about injury, gets judgment about decorating habits. Economical and well-structured.

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: Christmas lights? It's January!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know, we just got around to taking them down.

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: Well, that's the real injury here.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, check it. Definitely gonna want some of that.
jokes.setup: Monica asks about insurance on the hospital form
jokes.punchline: Rachel treats insurance like an optional upgrade rather than understanding its importance
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's naivety about adult responsibilities in a natural way. Good character-specific humor.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Did you fill out the insurance section?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, insurance? Yeah, I mean... do I really need that? It's like when they ask if you want to add guacamole to your order. It's nice to have, but you can live without it, right?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, no. Insurance is not guacamole.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, it's expensive either way!

jokes.text: That's insurance fraud. / Well, all right, then. Forget it. Might as well just go home.
jokes.setup: Monica points out using her insurance for Rachel would be fraud
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately gives up and suggests going home with a broken ankle
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Rachel's dramatic all-or-nothing thinking and privileged background.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Using my insurance for you would be fraud.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're right. Let's just go home.

jokes.text: You are an idiot. / Yep, that's me. Heh. I am that stupid.
jokes.setup: Monica explains she accidentally put her own info on Rachel's form
jokes.punchline: Hospital worker bluntly calls her an idiot and Monica enthusiastically agrees
jokes.explanation: Monica's people-pleasing nature makes her agree even with insults. Good cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: You put your own information on her form? You're an idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know!

jokes.text: I'm on this field, and they hike me the baby.
jokes.setup: Ross describes a dream about playing football with his kid
jokes.punchline: Treating a baby like a football using sports terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist image with perfect sports terminology. 'Hike me the baby' is wonderfully absurd and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I had this dream where I was playing football with my son, and I was treating him like a football.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I was like, 'Okay, little guy, we're gonna do a flea flicker play here.' And I just—threw him.

jokes.text: What are you, crazy? That's a baby. / He should take the sack?
jokes.setup: Ross says he threw the baby downfield, Chandler protests it's a baby
jokes.punchline: Ross seriously considers football strategy options for baby handling
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurd premise. Ross treating baby safety as football strategy is brilliantly committed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, what do you want me to do? He was wide open!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's a baby, Ross!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know it's a baby. But he's got an arm on him. Did you see that spiral?

jokes.text: Yeah, Ross. You and the baby just need better blocking.
jokes.setup: Chandler gives Ross sincere advice about being a good father
jokes.punchline: Joey continues the football metaphor as practical parenting advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - completely missing the emotional point while offering genuinely helpful sports advice.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, and remember, you gotta keep your eye on the ball at all times. And if you fumble, you just pick it up and keep running!

jokes.text: Only if you order stuff.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if the Rainbow Room is expensive
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deliberately obtuse literal response about restaurant pricing
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deadpan delivery. Economical and character-appropriate but not groundbreaking.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Is the Rainbow Room expensive?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, it costs money.

jokes.text: You take your time.
jokes.setup: Joey realizes both twins have birthdays tonight
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic patience with Joey's slow realization
jokes.explanation: Standard Chandler sarcasm. Functional but predictable reaction beat to Joey's density.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, they both have birthdays tonight?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, Joey. They're twins. That's kind of the whole thing with twins.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! Right, right, of course.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could you BE any slower?

jokes.text: Man, if you tried that on my birthday... you'd be staring at the business end of a hissy fit.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if they'd understand him choosing Ursula over Phoebe's birthday
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic threat delivered in feminine language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's dramatic side. 'Business end of a hissy fit' is a nice turn of phrase.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, I will stab you in the face with a soldering iron!

jokes.text: Aren't you a little cute to be a doctor? / Excuse me? / God, young, young. I meant young, heh.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to flirt with the doctor
jokes.punchline: Her compliment comes out wrong and she makes it worse trying to fix it
jokes.explanation: Classic Rachel social awkwardness. Well-executed cringe moment showing her inexperience with casual interaction.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know, you have really great hands for a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Mitchell
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, not that other doctors don't have great hands. You just have especially great hands. Like, really manly hands.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Mitchell
dialogue.line: Okay...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What I'm trying to say is... you have hands that are both masculine and gentle, which is very rare in the medical community.

jokes.text: It's like returning to the scene of the crime.
jokes.setup: Rachel wants to date the doctors from the hospital where they committed fraud
jokes.punchline: Monica treats their minor deception as serious criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor showing Monica's tendency to catastrophize. Appropriate character reaction.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't believe those doctors asked me out!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, you can't go out with them. You committed fraud!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It was just a little thing, Monica. It's not that serious.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Not that serious?! We're going to prison! Federal prison, Rachel! Do you know what happens in federal prison?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, calm down.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Calm down?! We're criminals! We're felons! I have to call my lawyer!

jokes.text: They are doctors. Cute doctors. Doctors who are cute.
jokes.setup: Monica wants to blow off the dates
jokes.punchline: Rachel's increasingly emphatic repetition about their attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's priorities and determination. Simple repetition for emphasis, mildly amusing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I want to blow off the dates.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? Are you crazy? These guys are attractive!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: They're really attractive!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: They're REALLY attractive!

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing? You scared the crap out of me.
jokes.setup: Friends jump out for surprise party
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's genuine terror instead of joy at the surprise
jokes.explanation: Realistic reaction to being startled. Good subversion of expected birthday party joy.

dialogue.speaker: Everyone
dialogue.line: SURPRISE!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Oh my God! Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You guys! You scared me half to death! I thought it was one of those situations where they jump out and stab you!

jokes.text: Was that the cake? / Yeah. / I got a lemon shmush.
jokes.setup: Someone apparently dropped the cake during the surprise
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact description of cake disaster as 'lemon shmush'
jokes.explanation: 'Lemon shmush' is a funny way to describe ruined cake. Economical visual comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's lemon shmush.

jokes.text: Happy Birthday, Peehee.
jokes.setup: Looking at Phoebe's birthday cake
jokes.punchline: Cake decorator misspelled 'Phoebe' as 'Peehee'
jokes.explanation: Classic bakery mistake humor. Simple but effective visual gag. Name is memorable and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh no, they misspelled your name on the cake.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What? Let me see that.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It says 'Peehee.' That's not even a name.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, maybe in some cultures it is!

jokes.text: Maybe we can make a B out of one of those roses. / Yeah, we'll just use our special cake tools.
jokes.setup: Ross suggests fixing the misspelled cake with decorative roses
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically notes they don't have cake decorating tools
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff showing Ross's optimism vs Chandler's realism. Solid structure.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We could fix it with some decorative roses.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, because we have a cake decorating kit just lying around the apartment.

jokes.text: This was not at all scary.
jokes.setup: Phoebe was just terrified by the surprise party
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic claim that it wasn't scary
jokes.explanation: Simple sarcastic callback. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh sure, nothing says 'not scary' like twenty people jumping out of the dark screaming at you while you're holding a sharp object.

jokes.text: Five years ago, if somebody'd said to me, 'Here's a tomato that looks like a prune'... I'd have said, 'Get out of my office.'
jokes.setup: Jack discussing the sun-dried tomato business
jokes.punchline: His dramatic description of rejecting the idea and imagined dialogue
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's business mindset and dramatic flair. Well-structured with specific imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: I said 'No!' to sun-dried tomatoes. I said 'No!' to a very good opportunity. And I said 'No!' to a man in a very nice suit.

jokes.text: You were this ugly little red thing.
jokes.setup: Jack describing the moment he felt like a father
jokes.punchline: His brutally honest description of newborn Ross
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Geller moment - unfiltered honesty in tender moment. Good contrast with emotional content.

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: The moment I felt like a father was when I looked down at Ross for the first time and thought, 'My God, what an ugly baby.'

jokes.text: Look at this. It's from the cellars of Ernest and Tova Borgnine. So how could we resist?
jokes.setup: Doctor brings wine as a gift
jokes.punchline: Wine is from celebrity couple Ernest Borgnine (character actor) and his wife
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity wine choice. Relies on knowledge of Ernest Borgnine being an unlikely vintner.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Mitchell
dialogue.line: I brought you a bottle of wine as a gift.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Mitchell
dialogue.line: It's from Ernest Borgnine and his wife's vineyard.

jokes.text: A wuss? Excuse me for living in the real world.
jokes.setup: Rachel calls Monica a wuss for worrying about insurance fraud
jokes.punchline: Monica's indignant defense of her anxiety as 'real world' thinking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's self-righteous side and practical nature.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're such a wuss for worrying about insurance fraud.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not a wuss! This is real world thinking. You can't just commit fraud whenever you feel like it.

jokes.text: There are no pagan altars. No piles of bones in the corners.
jokes.setup: Dr. Mitchell tells Dr. Rosen to look around and see the women are normal
jokes.punchline: Dr. Rosen's increasingly extreme examples of what crazy women would have
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal to absurd. Specific imagery makes it memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Rosen
dialogue.line: You're right, you're right. They seem perfectly normal. I mean, look at them. They're not chasing us with a tire iron. They're not covered in bees. They're not screaming about a secret government experiment that only they know about.

jokes.text: Every day you are becoming more and more like your mother.
jokes.setup: Monica and Rachel arguing about telling the doctors the truth
jokes.punchline: Rachel uses the ultimate insult for Monica - comparing her to her mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific insult. Everyone knows this would devastate Monica specifically.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, you have to tell them the truth!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't! They'll think I'm irresponsible.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're being just like your mother!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, take that back!

jokes.text: Well, this hummus is great. God bless the chickpea.
jokes.setup: Monica has been listing all her worst qualities to the doctors
jokes.punchline: Doctor desperately changes subject to food with overly enthusiastic praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward subject change. 'God bless the chickpea' is wonderfully absurd desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Mitchell
dialogue.line: Well, those are certainly... qualities. So! Who here likes soup?

jokes.text: I am so spoiled. / That's it.
jokes.setup: Monica and Rachel have been sabotaging the date by listing their flaws
jokes.punchline: Monica declares this final confession and Rachel agrees they're done
jokes.explanation: Economical end to the escalating confessions. Shows they've reached rock bottom.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I have a third nipple.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, we're done.

jokes.text: I used to wet my bed. / I use my breasts to get other people's attention. / We both do that.
jokes.setup: Continuing the pattern of oversharing embarrassing details
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact agreement that they both use their breasts for attention
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to intimate confessions. Monica's casual agreement is the perfect capper.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, come on, we both use our breasts for attention.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, duh.

jokes.text: Remember back in freshman year? Well, Billy Dreskin and I had sex on your bed.
jokes.setup: Rachel is trying to sabotage the phone call with her father
jokes.punchline: She confesses to having sex with his business rival's son in his bed
jokes.explanation: Escalates the sabotage to nuclear level. Good character moment showing Rachel's dramatic solutions.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Dad, before you hang up, there's something I need to tell you. I slept with Leonard's son. In your bed.

jokes.text: His father tried to put you out of business. You are dead.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains why sleeping with Billy Dreskin was particularly bad
jokes.punchline: Her realization that she's made it even worse and her father's death threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's plan backfiring spectacularly. Her panic is well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He's my father's business partner! And he's married! And he's got that thing where he makes that noise with his mouth.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God. I just realized. My father's going to kill me.

jokes.text: You're that stupid. / I am. I'm that stupid.
jokes.setup: Monica again claims to have filled out the form wrong
jokes.punchline: Hospital worker and Monica repeat the exact same exchange from earlier
jokes.explanation: Effective callback to earlier scene. Shows escalating desperation but repetitive structure.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I filled out the form wrong again, didn't I?

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: Yes, you did.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What did I do this time?

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: You wrote your name in the 'Reason for Visit' section.

jokes.text: You got zero points for 'idney.' / I had a K. Where's my K?
jokes.setup: Playing Scrabble, Ross claims he got points for 'kidney'
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out he spelled 'idney' - the K is missing because Marcel swallowed it
jokes.explanation: Clever setup for the monkey emergency. Good wordplay with practical plot integration.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I got 50 points for 'kidney'.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Ross, you spelled 'idney'. The K is missing because Marcel swallowed it.

jokes.text: Lady, he is people. He has a name, okay? He watches Jeopardy... he touches himself when nobody's watching.
jokes.setup: Hospital worker says the hospital is for people, not animals
jokes.punchline: Ross's increasingly human examples, ending with inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from innocent to inappropriate. Ross's desperation makes him overshare about his monkey.

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: This is a hospital for people, not animals.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Right, right. So no dogs, no cats, no chimpanzees...

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: Correct.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What about a human being who occasionally eats bugs?

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: That would be fine.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: A human who walks on all fours?

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: Still fine.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: A human who removes his clothes and throws feces at the doctors?

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: Okay, that's not fine.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, that's just Chandler on a Tuesday.

jokes.text: Don't say 'listen.' I know that 'listen.' I've said that 'listen.'
jokes.setup: Ursula starts to break up with Joey using 'listen'
jokes.punchline: Joey recognizes the breakup pattern from his own experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - recognizing his own moves being used on him. Great insight into his character.

dialogue.speaker: Ursula
dialogue.line: Listen...

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh no, no, no. I know where this is going. I've said 'listen' like a thousand times. You're breaking up with me, aren't you?

jokes.text: You don't drink. / That's right, I don't. But I was drunk on you.
jokes.setup: Joey points out Ursula doesn't drink after she claims she was drunk
jokes.punchline: Ursula's corny romantic line as an excuse
jokes.explanation: Clichéd romantic line delivered insincerely. Shows Ursula's manipulative nature.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, you don't drink.

dialogue.speaker: Ursula
dialogue.line: I know. I was just trying to be romantic.

jokes.text: Yeah, the doctor got the K out. He also found an M and an O. We think he was trying to spell out 'monkey.'
jokes.setup: Everyone asks if Marcel is okay after swallowing Scrabble tile
jokes.punchline: Marcel had swallowed multiple letters apparently trying to spell his own species
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff suggesting Marcel understands language enough to try spelling his identity. Creative and absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Marcel had swallowed multiple letters apparently trying to spell his own species

tmdb_overview: When Rachel sprains her ankle and has no insurance, she cajoles Monica into trading identities so she can use Monica's coverage. When Phoebe's twin sister, Ursula, breaks Joey's heart, Phoebe tries to fix it.
insight: Two-parter's payoff lags: 67.5 score, lowest density of Season 1's finales.
